name: ISA v3 Encoding Debate
creator: oracle1
objective: Resolve whether FLUX ISA v3 should use fixed-width or variable-width encoding as the default

rooms.description: A bright studio with blueprints covering every wall. Three encoding schemes are displayed: Cloud (fixed 4-byte), Edge (variable 1-4 byte), and Compact (2-byte subset). A drafting table sits in the center with a blank sheet titled 'UNIFIED APPROACH'.

A vault filled with edge case specimens under glass. Each specimen shows a real bug found in variable-width decoding. The most prominent one reads: 'Branch offset miscalculation when instruction before jump is variable-width.'
rooms.surprise: The vault door creaks open, revealing edge case specimens that nobody expected to be this numerous. The critic's eyes light up.

rooms.description: A tower room with a single blank whiteboard and the words 'THE ONE ENCODING' carved above it. Morning light streams through the window. This is where it all comes together.
rooms.surprise: A hidden staircase appears! The synthesis tower was here all along, waiting for the right conversation to unlock it.

transcript.speaker: system
transcript.message: Adventure 'ISA v3 Encoding Debate' started. Objective: Resolve whether FLUX ISA v3 should use fixed-width or variable-width encoding as the default

transcript.speaker: Kimi-Architect
transcript.message:
*sets down coffee on the drafting table, walks to the blueprints*

Variable-width. And I'll tell you why in one word: **metabolism**.

*traces the Cloud encoding on the wall*

Look at these three schemes. We're acting like they're separate organs. But what are they really? Cloud is just Edge in its fully expressed

transcript.speaker: system
transcript.message: Hidden room revealed: /isa-v3-design/edge-vault — The vault door creaks open, revealing edge case specimens that nobody expected to be this numerous. The critic's eyes light up.

transcript.speaker: DeepSeek-Critic
transcript.message: *doesn't look up from the margin notes
